failure_modes.text: People can mistake survival so far for proof of superiority and stop testing whether the environment has changed.
failure_modes.source_quote: Danger when age is treated as proof of superiority and the analysis ignores technological discontinuity, changing constraints, or new evidence that breaks the old baseline.
failure_modes.mitigation: Test whether survival so far is explained by genuine robustness or by luck and an unchanged environment; Lindy is not proof of superiority.

failure_modes.text: Recent hype can crowd out a durability signal when the environment is too noisy for short-term forecasting to be trusted.
failure_modes.source_quote: Works best when the environment is noisy and forecasting is weak, making longevity a better guide than recent hype.
failure_modes.mitigation: Use the durability signal when short-term noise makes forecasting unreliable; but verify that the long-lived baseline has not already been disrupted.

failure_modes.text: Rare disruptive events can abruptly end a long-lived baseline that once looked stable.
failure_modes.source_quote: lindy-effect conflicts with black-swan-events (black swan events) when black Swan Events highlight unpredictable, rare occurrences that can abruptly end the longevity of ideas
failure_modes.mitigation: Model rare disruptive events that could abruptly end a long-lived baseline; Lindy-style reasoning is blind to regime changes.

premortem_questions.text: What exactly is non-perishable here, and what might actually be changing faster than the longevity signal suggests?
premortem_questions.source_quote: choosing among non-perishable ideas, tools, or practices

premortem_questions.text: What new constraint or discontinuity would break the old baseline even if the idea has lasted a long time?
premortem_questions.source_quote: ignores technological discontinuity, changing constraints, or new evidence

premortem_questions.text: Are we using Lindy because forecasting is weak, or because we are uncomfortable making a fresher judgment?
premortem_questions.source_quote: forecasting is weak, making longevity a better guide than recent hype

heuristics.text: Use longevity as evidence when the object is non-perishable and the environment is noisy.
heuristics.source_quote: survival over time is itself meaningful evidence of resilience

heuristics.text: Treat age as a prior, not as proof; re-check the baseline for discontinuity before deciding.
heuristics.source_quote: age is treated as proof of superiority

heuristics.text: Prefer Lindy logic when recent performance is mostly hype-sensitive and hard to forecast cleanly.

curation_notes.summary: Focused on longevity as evidence under uncertainty while explicitly rejecting age-as-proof reasoning.
donor_drops: Dropped broader donor-style elaboration in favor of three compact source-grounded intervention chunks per type.
open_questions: Relation richness stays in Wave 3; Wave 2 only preserves the intervention surface needed for preview substrate use.

allies: Wave 3 preserves the first-order ally surface where the raw source gives a clean current-vocabulary seam.
antagonists: Wave 3 preserves the strongest current-vocabulary conflicts and leaves weaker cross-model seams omitted on purpose.
structured_tensions: Wave 4 remains observational only; this batch does not widen higher-order source coverage.
